schemeContent.benefits_md: - Payable for 11 & 12 class girl students: Rs. 3,000/- each year on the day of Basant Panchami.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The benefit of Gargi Puraskar Yojana is given every year to the beneficiary girl child of Rajasthan on the day of Basant Panchami. This time also the beneficiary girl students were taken on a tour of the girl students on the occasion of Basant Panchami very year.

children.text: Through the Gargi Puraskar Yojana, girls are given incentives from the government to move forward in the field of education. Girls of all classes can take advantage of the scheme. The amount of this scheme will be given every year on Basant Panchami.

The girl students who will get the benefit of the scheme in class 10th will be required to enrol in the school in classes 11th and 12th. And the government will give the amount to the beneficiary girl child through a check, so it is mandatory for the girl child to have a bank account. You can apply for the scheme online.
Payable for 11 & 12 class girl students: Rs. 3,000/- each year on the day of Basant Panchami.
Scholarship Application online through School Principal on
Click on "Balika Shiksha Protsahan" or click
Select the scheme and click on apply button.
Fill out the required details and process further.

applicationProcess.process_md:
1. Scholarship Application online through School Principal on [Shaladarpan Portal](https://rajshaladarpan.nic.in/SD2/Home/Public2/Default.aspx).1. Click on "Balika Shiksha Protsahan" or click [here](https://rajshaladarpan.nic.in/SD2/Home/BSF/Index.aspx). 1. Select the scheme and click on apply button. 
1. Now select the scheme name. 
1. Fill out the required details and process further.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The student must have secured minimum 75% marks in class 10th.
1. The girl student should be studying regularly in class 11 or 12.
1. There is no restriction on income, occupation and caste etc. of the parents of the student.

children.text: The student must have secured minimum 75% marks in class 10th.
The girl student should be studying regularly in class 11 or 12.
There is no restriction on income, occupation and caste etc. of the parents of the student.
